General Valerii Zaluzhnyi on the Impossibility of Operational-Level Tasks Due to Technological Progress

2 min read · thread (X/Twitter) · 2026-04-29
zaluzhnyiukraine-warmilitary-strategyoperational-arttechnological-progressmodern-warfaredefense-innovationbattlefield-transparencyukraine-armed-forces
Source: https://x.com/revishvilig/status/2049561109028339941?s=46&t=OHfXTbjdV4tiD3nnG1ls...

Summary

In this thread, Giorgi Revishvili shares remarks from General Valerii Zaluzhnyi — former Commander-in-Chief of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and current Ukrainian Ambassador to the UK — arguing that scientific and technological progress has fundamentally altered the nature of warfare. Zaluzhnyi contends that it has become impossible to carry out traditional operational-level tasks, a claim that strikes at the heart of conventional military doctrine built around large-scale maneuver, surprise, and deep operational breakthroughs. Zaluzhnyi's assessment draws directly from Ukraine's battlefield experience against Russia, where ubiquitous surveillance (drones, satellites, signals intelligence) and precision strike capabilities have created a condition of near-total battlefield transparency. This environment makes massing forces for decisive offensive operations extraordinarily costly, leading to attritional, positional warfare reminiscent of World War I rather than the mobile campaigns envisioned in post-Cold War doctrine. The implications of Zaluzhnyi's argument extend well beyond Ukraine. If operational-level maneuver is truly obsolete in its classical form, Western militaries — whose doctrines rely heavily on combined-arms maneuver at operational and strategic depth — face a profound conceptual crisis. Zaluzhnyi's perspective, shaped by direct command experience in the most intense conventional war in decades, demands serious engagement from defense planners and strategists worldwide.

Key Takeaways

  • Zaluzhnyi argues that technological advances have made traditional operational-level military tasks effectively impossible in modern warfare.
  • Ubiquitous ISR (drones, satellites, electronic surveillance) has created battlefield transparency that prevents the concentration and surprise required for large-scale maneuver.
  • Ukraine's war experience demonstrates a reversion to attritional, positional warfare rather than the mobile combined-arms operations envisioned in modern doctrine.
  • The claim challenges foundational Western military concepts that depend on operational maneuver for decisive outcomes.
  • Zaluzhnyi's authority on this topic is uniquely credible given his direct command of the largest conventional war in Europe since 1945.
  • Defense establishments globally need to reassess force structure, doctrine, and procurement in light of these battlefield realities.
  • The thread underscores an emerging consensus among practitioners that technology has shifted the offense-defense balance decisively toward the defense at the operational level.

Full Text / Transcript

General Valerii Zaluzhnyi, former Commander-in-Chief of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and current Ambassador to the UK: Due to scientific and technological progress, it has become impossible, regardless of what others may claim, to carry out operational-level tasks. 1/12 https://t.co/7XYtf97QSd
